meaning: n.<口><<德布雷特英国贵族年鉴>>(初版由英国出版家 JohnDebrett 于1803年编纂出版)
examples: Debrett's etiquette adviser Liz Wyse says that both of them must come out.
The authority on etiquette, Debrett's, says traditionally, if you were not known by the bride's mother, you did not get an invitation.
"How one behaves when one meets the Queen is fairly self-evident and obvious, " said David Williamson, co-editor of Debrett's (the last word in social etiquette).
